title: Quiz: Monte Carlo Simulation
description: Test your understanding of Monte Carlo methods

questions.question: What is the Metropolis acceptance probability for a move with ΔE > 0?
questions.explanation: For uphill moves (ΔE > 0), the acceptance probability is exp(-ΔE/kT). This allows occasional uphill moves, enabling the system to escape local minima and properly sample the Boltzmann distribution.

questions.question: What is the target acceptance rate in Metropolis MC?
questions.explanation: A 20-50% acceptance rate balances between large moves (exploration) and accepted moves (efficiency). Too high means steps are too small; too low means most moves are rejected.

questions.question: Which ensemble allows particle number to fluctuate?
questions.explanation: The grand canonical ensemble (μVT) fixes chemical potential μ, volume V, and temperature T, allowing the number of particles N to fluctuate through insertion/deletion moves.

questions.question: Monte Carlo provides real dynamical trajectories of molecular motion.
questions.explanation: False. Monte Carlo generates configurations according to the Boltzmann distribution but does not provide real dynamics. The sequence of configurations is a stochastic walk, not a physical trajectory.

questions.question: What is 'detailed balance' in Monte Carlo?

options.text: Equal numbers of accepted and rejected moves
Equal step sizes in all directions

questions.explanation: Detailed balance requires that the probability flow between any two states is equal at equilibrium: P(x)T(x→x') = P(x')T(x'→x). This ensures the Metropolis algorithm samples the correct distribution.

questions.question: Which are advantages of Monte Carlo over MD? (Select all)

options.text: Can use non-physical moves that would violate dynamics
Easier to implement variable particle number

questions.explanation: MC can use non-physical moves (like particle insertion) and easily handles variable N ensembles. However, MC samples are correlated (not independent) and MC does not provide real dynamics.
